what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard item that presents a capability audit table titled 'The FREE-vs-SHIM ledger'. It lists features of an Obsidian-based workflow (full-text search, properties panel, Bases/Dataview board view, graph view, backlinks, logical document assembly, canvas/plugins/sync, and editing items back) and marks each as either working natively on the project's markdown/YAML data ('Free'), requiring an L2 shim, or being an L3 dangerous operation. It argues that only the typed-edge graph/backlinks and the assembled-document view need shims, while everything tabular/textual is free today.
observations: The file uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, channel, author_session, title, created, updated, and a links list-of-dicts.
The body is a markdown table with columns Capability, Status on OUR data, and Why.
Each 'Why' cell is tagged as [Inferred] or [Observed] to indicate the basis of the claim.
Graph view and backlinks are marked 'NOT free -> L2 shim' because Obsidian only follows [[wikilinks]], tags, and wikilink-properties, not a YAML list-of-dicts.
The file cites an observation from cc_board.py:159-172 and notes that .base files exist in /home/tim/vaults/Obsidian Builder/.
A 'single load-bearing line' summary states that only the typed-edge graph and the assembled-document view need a shim.
Editing items back is labeled 'L3, dangerous' due to two-writer and YAML-shape fragility, with details deferred to §5.
standouts: The 'single load-bearing line' that narrows the required shim scope to just the typed-edge graph and the assembled-document view.
The explicit reason that Obsidian cannot natively walk a YAML list-of-dicts for graph edges.
The distinction between [Inferred] and [Observed] evidence for each capability claim.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item format with board:// address and YAML frontmatter.
Uses a markdown table for a capability-vs-status matrix.
References external code locations and local filesystem paths as supporting evidence.
Uses project-specific shorthand labels: Free, SHIM, L2, L3.
concerns: Most 'Free' claims are marked [Inferred] rather than empirically demonstrated in this file.
The 'L3, dangerous' claim about editing back is asserted but the supporting §5 content is not present here.
The absolute path /home/tim/vaults/Obsidian Builder/ is environment-specific and may not generalize.
Terms like L2, L3, and SHIM are not defined within the file itself.
notes: A concise capability audit that argues most Obsidian features are free on the markdown/YAML ledger, with only graph/backlinks and assembled-document views needing a shim layer.
questions: What is the exact implementation of the L2 shim for graph view and assembled documents?
Where are the referenced §4 and §5 sections located?
How are the YAML links list-of-dicts generated and kept consistent with actual relationships?
What do L2 and L3 specifically denote in this project's layering model?
What is the content and role of the linked parent item board://item-4baaab55?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to deliver the FREE-vs-SHIM ledger, and the file does exactly that by enumerating capabilities and their shim status; purpose matches content.
apparent_themes: Maximize native Obsidian affordances before adding engineering.
intention_signals: The file appears to be scoping the engineering surface area for integrating a structured markdown ledger with Obsidian, aiming to show that most desired features come for free and only a narrow graph/assembly shim is needed.
novelty: The 'FREE-vs-SHIM' ledger framing and the strong conclusion that only two capabilities (typed-edge graph and assembled-document view) require shims, while the rest of the tabular/textual experience is already native.
contribution: It contributes a compact capability-to-shim mapping and an evidence-tagging convention ([Inferred]/[Observed]) that helps a fused system trace which claims are assumptions versus observations.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item presenting a FREE-vs-SHIM ledger that audits Obsidian capabilities against a markdown/YAML ledger, concluding that only graph/backlinks and assembled-document views require shims.
intention_for_embedding: To determine which Obsidian features work natively on the project's structured markdown data and which require a shim layer.
